- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "alternating-sport", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "Czech", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Secondary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Too young to vote, but interested in social issues", "Enjoys tinkering with gadgets and creating digital art"]}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: jolly-persian
logs.text:
> Because simply holding a Nazi flag and saying "I am a Nazi" is not justification in itself for being assaulted

That's not what I'm challenging here. You claimed that

>The term "Nazi" can vary in definition depending on the person you ask, and after our society agrees that it is okay to physically assault Nazis, the definition of the word "Nazi" can be watered down while the connotation of the original definition remains.

I'm asking if someone waving Nazi flags calling themself a Nazi, is a Nazi or no? Common sense says they are.

>No, I said "our society should not leave open such a gaping blind spot for violent people to exploit."

So why should society leave open a gaping blind spot for Nazis?

In your ideal world, Nazis could absolutely run for office, win elections, etc. Even if individual Nazis went around killing people- well you jail those people, but the Nazi party at large could continue to operate with no violence against them. If they won enough seats in government, of course, they could then overturn (or just ignore) *the very free speech/free assembly/free press laws* that allowed them to come to power, and throw anyone they wanted into concentration camps.

This isn't merely a hypothetical; it literally happened. Hitler spent a stint in jail; it didn't stop him. People who were willing to fight Nazis with violence (the original antifa) were given far harsher sentences than Nazis were. And what happened?

This seems like a much bigger "gaping blind spot for violent people to exploit" than your scenario, and one with historical precedent.

>Thousands of people died on 9/11. That doesn't mean I can punch someone in the face for saying "Al Qaeda is awesome"

Interesting question! Tbh, I don't care if you do. As it stands, the US government will gladly drone strike US citizens (and their children) [for running a YouTube channel supporting al-Qaeda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anwar_al-Awlaki). Most white Americans don't bat an eye at this type of stuff; why do they protest about the punching of Nazis? Because unlike al-Qaeda, those Nazis are their brethren.

>It's not negligible. These Antifa people show up in the hundreds every time Milo Yiannopolous, Ben Shapiro (both Jews), Gavin McInnes, and a lot of other conservative people (who have NEVER advocated genocide or anything close)

Milo consistently shills for the alt-right, aka Nazis. The fact that he is a Jew doesn't change that, and the opinion of many Nazis is that he is a "useful" Jew.

Ben is noxious but not Fascist (though he does basically shill for them); but he isn't attacked by antifa. You seem to label any violent leftist action as "antifa" when it isn't. Antifa is not just a blanket moniker for any violent leftist group.

McInnes is absolutely a Fascist. Go to the /r/proudboys subreddit if you don't believe me. They literally go around disrupting protests by indigenous peoples and regularly show up alongside Nazi-flag wavers at rallies like Unite the Right.

>They came to my college a year ago and I can tell you they are animals.

Actually, Ben, Gavin and Milo are animals for supporting people who want to murder me and anyone who looks like me.

>Not advocating violence against a group is not the same thing as open tolerance. Come on man, you know that.

So what concrete steps should we take against Nazis? Historically, the only thing that's worked is violence, or the threat of violence. And anyways any measure you take against Nazis is only as good as the guns you have to enforce it.

>I agree that not all are equally valid, but that doesn't stop different definitions from being used. My point is that if one person's definition is "all Trump supporters" and another person's is "a punchable person", and the former informs the latter that a person is a Nazi, it will lead to unnecessary violence.

The world is full of unnecessary violence; that's no reason to make blanket, self-contradictory maxims like "don't punch Nazis because no one knows what a Nazi is."

logs.name: alternating-sport
logs.text:
>That's not what I'm challenging here. You claimed that

You asked me if holding a Nazi flag and saying "I am a Nazi" makes one a Nazi. I said "it depends what else they are dong". You asked why. The reason is we don't know why they are doing that. It might be a social experiment, it might be a poor joke. Maybe a guy lost a bet and has to do that. People die all the time getting punched in the face. One time a co-worker's friend of mine got into a scuffle with someone and the guy sucker punched him and he fell and cracked his head on the sidewalk - dead. That's why I take this shit seriously. 

>So why should society leave open a gaping blind spot for Nazis?

It's called lesser of 2 evils. Allowing idiots to say stupid shit is less evil than giving violent people an excuse to be violent.

>In your ideal world, Nazis could absolutely run for office, win elections, etc

Nazis can and have run for office, but have never even come close to winning. Even on the off chance that a Nazi somehow manages to hold office somewhere, he or she is constrained by the constitution, which prevents them from doing most of the evil that the Nazis did. But this is getting *way* ahead of ourselves. 

>they could then overturn (or just ignore) the very free speech/free assembly/free press laws that allowed them to come to power, and throw anyone they wanted into concentration camps.

No they couldn't. First of all, the chances of an *actual* Nazi getting elected is just unbelievably low. I would bet my entire savings that it will never happen. But even if it did, we are talking about the *constitution* here. This isn't Germany. We have a better system than they do. In order to change the constitution, you need 2/3 of *both* houses of congress to agree to the changes, and the 3/4 of the states to vote on it. The chances of that happening are so unbelievably thin. It's not worth punching "Nazis" in the face to prevent this unbelievably unlikely thing from happening. It's ridiculous. The only way Nazis have even a remote chance of taking over the country is through violence, in which case I already justified using violence against them in the bottom of the OP.

>Tbh, I don't care if you do.

This is pretty appalling to be honest. I absolutely condemn violence against people who say "Al-Qaeda is awesome" for the same reasons I condemn violence against Nazis.

>Most white Americans don't bat an eye at this type of stuff

I would say most white Americans don't *know* about this stuff, and even if they did, can't do anything about it.

>Milo consistently shills for the alt-right

No, no no. This is completely wrong. [He is hated by the alt-right. ](http://www.dailystormer.com/stormer-book-club-crusade-the-final-solution-to-the-milo-problem/). He has also repeatedly denounced the alt-right and in his book refuted numerous articles (which Wikipedia uses as sources) linking him to the alt-right.

>Ben is noxious but not Fascists; but he isn't attacked by antifa. You seem to label any violent leftist action as "antifa" when it isn't. Antifa is not just a blanket moniker for any violent leftist group.

He was just attacked this past weekend by Antifa. Also, it doesn't matter what they call themselves. This post is for anyone who believes in using violence to censor "Nazis".

>McInnes is absolutely a Fascist. Go to the /r/proudboys subreddit if you don't believe me. They literally go around disrupting protests by indigenous peoples and regularly show up alongside Nazi-flag wavers at rallies like Unite the Right.

This is also wrong, but I'm glad you brought up the proud boys. [Nazis have infiltrated the proud boys](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=o8DhmEunckg&t=13s), and no proud boys went to the unite the right thing. Gavin has also repeatedly condemned Nazis and alt-right, but that doesn't stop people from beating and [pepper spraying him and his supporters](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=izLJlkiTxVA).

>Actually, Ben, Gavin and Milo are animals for supporting people who want to murder me and anyone who looks like me.

Do you have any evidence that this is true?

>So what concrete steps should we take against Nazis?

Not voting for them. 

>that's no reason to make blanket, self-contradictory maxims like "don't punch Nazis because no one knows what a Nazi is."

This is a complete misrepresentation of what I said. I didn't say or imply that people don't know what a Nazi is. I said that different people have different definitions for what a Nazi is and that some definitions are looser than others.

logs.name: heather-crescendo
logs.text: Wow, this is getting heated. Seriously questioning the validity of people's opinions here. It's a messy situation. @alternating-sport you're painting a really complex picture. I think there's a lot of nuance being missed in this discussion. It's not as simple as "punch Nazis." There are real concerns about free speech and the potential for abuse. And @jolly-persian, while I understand the frustration with hate speech, I think demonizing entire groups of people isn't productive either. It's a really tricky issue.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Moderator: Let's keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand and refrain from personal attacks. Remember, we're here to discuss ideas, not to demonize individuals. Let's maintain a respectful and constructive conversation. @jolly-persian, @alternating-sport, @heather-crescendo, and all participants, please ensure your comments adhere to these guidelines."

logs.name: jolly-persian
logs.text: Okay, wow this is getting intense! It's so frustrating when people try to simplify complex issues like this. It feels like there's no room for nuance when people are so quick to judge. @alternating-sport, I think you might be overlooking some really important aspects of free speech and accountability. It's not about condoning violence, but about understanding the motivations behind it. And @heather-crescendo is right, demonizing entire groups isn't helpful at all. It's so exhausting trying to navigate conversations like this.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Moderator: @jolly-persian, thank you for your input. Let's ensure we keep the conversation constructive and avoid escalating the tension. Remember, the goal is to understand different perspectives and engage in a respectful dialogue. Let's continue to focus on the topic and avoid personal attacks."

logs.name: paper-winning
logs.text: Seriously?! This is getting ridiculous. People are jumping to conclusions and throwing around loaded language. It feels like nobody wants to actually have a productive conversation anymore. @alternating-sport, you need to admit you haven't considered other angles. And @heather-crescendo, chill. Demonizing anyone isn't gonna solve anything. It's just lazy thinking.
